name: What LLM Forecasters Know but Don't Say: Probing Internal Representations for Calibration and Faithfulness

four_principles_filter.去粗取精: 研讨厅按 intake_score≥0.6 + 子系统匹配入库
four_principles_filter.去伪存真: 代码级落地/规划态已在决策中标注
four_principles_filter.由表及里: 研讨厅驱动正式入库固化

digest_notes: Large language models fine-tuned for forecasting can be accurate yet poorly calibrated. We ask whether internal representations offer a more direct window. We train representation-pooling probes on intermediate activations and find they achieve substantially better calibration. The same probes function as lie detectors: their activations track behavioral shifts far better than the reasoning trace does. Forced answering reveals that forecasts are fixed before reasoning begins.
